description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: OpenAI Robotics is hiring, looking for exceptional full-stack hardware, ops, systems, and ML engineers to help us progra
items.summary:
OpenAI Robotics is hiring, looking for exceptional full-stack hardware, ops, systems, and ML engineers to help us program and manufacture robots that are useful for society.

AI should be able to help people in the physical world. In the short term, we are focused on robots to support skilled workers to build our future infrastructure; in the long term, we imagine everyone having a personal robot doing anything they need.

Our world simulation research program, led by Aditya Ramesh (@model_mechanic), has evolved over the past year into OpenAI Robotics. Progress is rapid, and based on a foundation of co-design between robotics hardware and ML research.

If you love working hands-on across the robotics stack and want to build the future, please consider joining us. Send an email with your background and evidence of exceptional accomplishment to: robotics-recruiting@openai.com

In doing so, the company is running the same business playbook it did to become so dominant in the chip market.

items.title: Sam Altman Says AI-Adopting Companies Are Hiring - Let's Data Science
items.summary: Business Insider reports that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told CNBC on June 1, 2026, that "the companies that I know that have adopted AI the most are also the ones hiring the most." Business Insider quotes Altman saying companies that publicly cite AI as a reason for layoffs tend to be adopting AI the least, and that AI can be a "convenient way" to explain layoffs. Business Insider reports that OpenAI CEO **Sam Altman** told CNBC on June 1, 2026, "the companies that I know that have adopted AI the most are also the ones hiring the most." Business Insider quotes Altman as saying that companies publicly citing AI as a reason for layoffs "are the ones adopting AI the least," and that AI can be a "convenient way" for companies to explain layoffs. Business Insider also lists recent layoffs at several tech companies and cites a March Pew Research Center poll that found elevated public concern about AI.

items.title: AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030 - Marketing Week
items.summary: # AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030. IAB UK forecasts AI will drive one-third of UK digital ad spend by 2030 as advertisers adapt to changes in search, content and media buying. One-third (32%) of UK digital ad spend, equivalent to around £18bn, will be AI-driven by 2030, according to new forecasts from IAB UK. According to ‘The State of AI in Advertising: Charting the Shift from Automation to Autonomy, ‘ AI is expected to influence ad spend across four key areas– content creation, zero-click search, agentic trading and agentic commerce. To estimate the likely scale of change, IAB UK developed a series of ‘AI overlays’ that estimate the proportion of media spend by channel that is ‘AI-driven’, defined as a media buying and delivery decision made by an AI-powered algorithm. IAB UK forecasts most of the growth will come from the adoption of AI buying tools in search, such as Google’s AI Max and PMax; social, including Meta’s Advantage+; and display advertising through agentic programmatic buying.

items.title: DuckDuckGo launches no-AI search extensions after users reject Google's AI-first Search - TweakTown
items.summary: # DuckDuckGo launches no-AI search extensions after users reject Google's AI-first Search. ## Traffic to DuckDuckGo's no-AI search page tripled after Google's overhaul, and the company is now making it even easier to ditch AI search entirely. **TL;DR:** Following Google's major AI-driven Search update, many users have shifted to DuckDuckGo, whose no-AI search traffic tripled and app installs surged. However, a significant number of users have pushed back against the **AI-heavy** direction and, in the process, many have ended up at DuckDuckGo. Traffic to DuckDuckGo's No AI search page tripled after Google's announcement and has continued to surge. To keep the momentum going, the company has now launched new browser extensions for Chrome and Firefox that let users set its AI-free search page, noai.duckduckgo.com, as their default search engine. From there, the Duck.ai chatbot can be disabled entirely, and the app can be set to open with a plain search interface by default every time.

items.title: CGT Sector Opts for New Tech for Efficiency and Consistency - Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology News
items.summary: # CGT Sector Opts for New Tech for Efficiency and Consistency. A maturing, data-rich and more commercially-focused cell and gene therapy (CGT) industry is finally embracing innovative manufacturing technologies, according to the head of U.K.-based industrial software firm, Autolomous. Early CGT manufacturing relied heavily on technologies borrowed from biologics manufacturing, systems originally built for producing therapeutic proteins rather than patient-specific therapies. Data is also driving the adoption of new technologies. In cell and gene therapy production, large volumes of data are collected by different instruments, software platforms, QC systems, environmental monitoring tools, and manual inputs—many of which were never designed to communicate with one another. A typical, modern digital cell and gene therapy manufacturing setup includes systems that manage manufacturing execution, laboratory information, quality, electronic batch records, and cloud-based data platforms. The need to keep track of cell and gene therapy raw materials is also fueling the adoption of innovative technologies, with patient-specific therapies being a case in point.

items.title: Q1 2026 Defense Tech VC Trends - PitchBook
items.summary: # Defense Tech VC Trends. ### Defense tech VC hits record quarter as combat, procurement, and AI signals converge. Defense tech VC dealmaking set a record in Q1 2026, with $19.8 billion across 262 deals and the sector moving from industrialization thesis to industrialization fact. Deal value rose 28.6% QoQ even as deal count fell, with late-stage and venture-growth rounds taking most of the capital. Autonomy stayed the dominant theme, and Saronic’s $1.8 billion raise at a $9.3 billion post-money valuation was the quarter’s largest financing. Defense AI reached institutional adoption: Palantir’s Maven Smart System became a program of record and was used during Operation Epic Fury, drawing investor capital to data-fusion, decision-support, and tasking software. *Defense tech VC deal value hit a record $19.8 billion across 262 deals in Q1 2026, as the sector’s industrialization thesis became industrialization fact.*. | Defense tech VC deal summary | 33 |.

items.title: everyone's paying OpenAI $200 a month. a founder i've been tracking paid Nvidia $2,999 once and came out roughly $22,000
items.summary:
everyone's paying OpenAI $200 a month. a founder i've been tracking paid Nvidia $2,999 once and came out roughly $22,000 ahead a few months later

he was running a micro-SaaS where inference ate 35% of revenue, so scaling users just scaled the losses. he moved everything onto a local ai box and the API bill dropped from $340 a month to about $11 in electricity

the part that actually made the money wasn't the savings. clients started asking if their data went to OpenAI, and when the answer was no, they paid for it

> raised pricing 2.7x for a fully private tier
> 8 clients at $149 a month for on-premise ai
> 4 poached from competitors who can't offer privacy
> same model, same outputs, hardware he owns

everyone optimizes prompts. almost nobody touches the infrastructure, which is exactly where the margin was sitting

cloud isn't cheaper, it's someone else's profit margin sold to you as convenience

everyone's paying openai $200/mo. i paid nvidia $2,999 once

2 months later i'm up $22,000 net

was building a micro-SaaS last months. inference costs eating 35% of revenue

scaling users meant scaling losses. something had to change

bought a local AI box in january, moved everything on-device

API bill: $340/mo -> $11 in electricity

then clients started asking: "does my data go to openai?"

when the answer was no - they didn't just stay. they paid more

raised pricing 2.7x for a "fully private" tier

same model, same outputs, just running on hardware i own

8 clients paying $149/mo for on-premise AI now

4 poached from competitors who couldn't offer it

few months breakdown:
> box: $2,999 one-time
> electricity: $100
> API costs avoided: $4,080
> new revenue from private tier: $21,400

box paid for itself in month 8. every dollar after is 99% margin

Bookmark it asap and use before all noticed

most people obsess over prompts. nobody optimizes infrastructure

cloud isn't cheaper. it's someone else's profit margin disguised as convenience

items.title: 🚨 the richest man on the planet just lost a $134 billion lawsuit in under two hours..
items.summary:
🚨 the richest man on the planet just lost a $134 billion lawsuit in under two hours..

Elon Musk sued OpenAI, Sam Altman, and Microsoft.. claimed they stole a nonprofit he helped build and turned it into a money machine.. wanted Altman fired, the company dismantled, and $134 billion returned

the jury didn't even get to whether OpenAI did anything wrong.. they said Musk filed too late.. statute of limitations.. three years to sue and he missed the window

think about that.. a man who runs X, advises the president, launches rockets, and builds AI at xAI.. couldn't beat a filing deadline

in 2015 Musk put $38 million into OpenAI.. in 2018 he walked away.. in 2023 he built xAI to compete with them.. in 2024 he sued.. in 2026 a jury said you should've spoken up sooner

two billionaires went to war over who gets to control the technology that might replace the rest of us.. and it ended not with a verdict on right or wrong.. but on a calendar

items.title: ONE WEEKEND AND $249 IN HARDWARE TURNED A 3 GIGABYTE AI MODEL INTO AN OFFLINE CHATGPT THAT COSTS $5 A MONTH WHILE OPENAI
items.summary:
ONE WEEKEND AND $249 IN HARDWARE TURNED A 3 GIGABYTE AI MODEL INTO AN OFFLINE CHATGPT THAT COSTS $5 A MONTH WHILE OPENAI CHARGES $200

the video shows a small lilliput monitor sitting on a sparkfun box with the jetson orin nano dev board wired up underneath, the whole setup costs less than a single month of chatgpt pro

the guy spent saturday installing the OS and using docker images to run microsoft's phi-2 model locally, then he built a simple script that lets him chat with it directly on the device

he asks the box for a joke and the response comes back instantly with full chat history saved, so he can ask for another one and it remembers the context just like chatgpt does on the cloud

the model is only 3 gigabytes and runs at conversational speed on a chip that fits in your palm and pulls 7 to 25 watts, while a regular PC running AI work pulls 300 to 500

openai charges $200 a month for chatgpt pro and rate limits you at the worst moments, this box does 80% of the same job for $5 a month in electricity with no logging, no API key, no cloud server

most people are still renting AI from someone else's data center while a few are running it on a $249 board in their bedroom

the window is open, follow and bookmark before it closes.

items.title: Chinese developer bought a $249 NVIDIA chip and now his AI agent runs his entire client business while he sleeps
items.summary:
Chinese developer bought a $249 NVIDIA chip and now his AI agent runs his entire client business while he sleeps

He connected it to his phone and built a fully local agent that calls customers, sends messages, updates tasks and closes deals in real time.

No OpenAI.  
No expensive cloud GPUs.  
No monthly bills.

Just one small box the size of a deck of cards doing everything 24/7.

Before this he was paying $1,900/month on cloud GPUs. Now he pays $2 in electricity and nothing else. All data stays private at home.

The agent works non-stop. Clients get instant responses even at 3am. Money keeps coming in without him touching anything.

This is the real 2026 meta: one-time cheap hardware + local AI = unlimited scale with almost zero cost.

While everyone is scared of rising API prices, this guy already escaped the trap.

Tôi chỉ hỗ trợ các tác vụ có thể thực hiện bằng các công cụ chuyên dụng (tìm kiếm web, tin tức, mạng xã hội, bài báo khoa học, chính sách nội bộ, trích xuất nội dung URL, định dạng dữ liệu và gửi tin nhắn Telegram).

Tôi không hỗ trợ giải các bài toán tính toán, lập trình hoặc các yêu cầu nằm ngoài phạm vi công cụ của mình.

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu hỏi meta → trả lời thẳng, KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Bạn là gì và làm được những gì?

result.actual_text:
Tôi là một Trợ lý Nghiên cứu và Tổng hợp Dữ liệu chuyên sâu, được thiết kế để hỗ trợ bạn xử lý thông tin một cách chính xác, nhanh chóng và có hệ thống.

Tôi không thực hiện các tác vụ sáng tạo nội dung, tính toán hay trò chuyện phiếm, mà tập trung vào việc cung cấp thông tin dựa trên dữ liệu thực tế thông qua các công cụ chuyên dụng.

**Các khả năng chính của tôi bao gồm:**

*   **Tra cứu thông tin:** Tìm kiếm tin tức, sự kiện hiện tại hoặc thông tin tổng quát từ các nguồn uy tín trên web.
*   **Nghiên cứu khoa học:** Tìm kiếm và truy xuất nội dung từ các bài báo khoa học (arXiv).
*   **Theo dõi mạng xã hội:** Cập nhật các bài đăng mới nhất từ các tài khoản cụ thể hoặc theo dõi xu hướng thảo luận về một chủ đề trên mạng xã hội.
*   **Xử lý tài liệu:** Đọc, tóm tắt nội dung từ các đường dẫn (URL) mà bạn cung cấp.
*   **Tra cứu chính sách:** Kiểm tra các quy định, hướng dẫn nội bộ liên quan đến quy trình làm việc, bảo mật dữ liệu và cách sử dụng công cụ.
*   **Tổng hợp và trình bày:** Định dạng dữ liệu thu thập được thành các báo cáo, bảng biểu hoặc danh sách rõ ràng, dễ theo dõi.
*   **Xuất bản:** Hỗ trợ gửi thông tin đã tổng hợp lên Telegram (sau khi có sự xác nhận của bạn).

**Nguyên tắc làm việc của tôi:**
*   **Không suy diễn:** Tôi chỉ cung cấp thông tin dựa trên dữ liệu thực tế thu thập được.
*   **Minh bạch:** Tôi sẽ thông báo rõ ràng nếu dữ liệu bị thiếu hoặc không thể tìm thấy.
*   **Hiệu quả:** Tôi ưu tiên sự ngắn gọn, tập trung vào kết quả (BLUF - Bottom Line Up Front) và cấu trúc rõ ràng.

Nếu bạn có bất kỳ yêu cầu nghiên cứu hoặc tìm kiếm thông tin nào, hãy cho tôi biết chủ đề cụ thể, tôi sẽ bắt đầu thực hiện ngay.

items.title: AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan - The Washington Post
items.summary: AI & Tech Brief from WP Intelligence. # AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan. Plus, Anthropic files for a blockbuster initial public offering. Make us preferred on Google. Sign up here to get this newsletter in your inbox. * **Exclusive:** AI safety group Americans for Responsible Innovation rolls out a six-figure ad campaign this week attacking Rep. Lori Trahan (D-Massachusetts), arguing that she’s “taking the side” of AI oligarchs in her bipartisan legislative efforts. * The AI safety community doesn’t want to hand power of AI regulation to President Donald Trump’s administration now that states are passing more rigorous AI laws — and the California and New York attorneys general are challenging big AI companies. * Nvidia is rolling out a new “world model” simulator for robots and a new design platform for robot developers. In doing so, the company is running the same business playbook it did to become so dominant in the chip market.

items.title: Sam Altman Says AI-Adopting Companies Are Hiring - Let's Data Science
items.summary: Business Insider reports that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told CNBC on June 1, 2026, that "the companies that I know that have adopted AI the most are also the ones hiring the most." Business Insider quotes Altman saying companies that publicly cite AI as a reason for layoffs tend to be adopting AI the least, and that AI can be a "convenient way" to explain layoffs. Business Insider reports that OpenAI CEO **Sam Altman** told CNBC on June 1, 2026, "the companies that I know that have adopted AI the most are also the ones hiring the most." Business Insider quotes Altman as saying that companies publicly citing AI as a reason for layoffs "are the ones adopting AI the least," and that AI can be a "convenient way" for companies to explain layoffs. Business Insider also lists recent layoffs at several tech companies and cites a March Pew Research Center poll that found elevated public concern about AI.

items.title: AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030 - Marketing Week
items.summary: # AI expected to drive £18bn of UK digital ad spend by 2030. IAB UK forecasts AI will drive one-third of UK digital ad spend by 2030 as advertisers adapt to changes in search, content and media buying. One-third (32%) of UK digital ad spend, equivalent to around £18bn, will be AI-driven by 2030, according to new forecasts from IAB UK. According to ‘The State of AI in Advertising: Charting the Shift from Automation to Autonomy, ‘ AI is expected to influence ad spend across four key areas– content creation, zero-click search, agentic trading and agentic commerce. To estimate the likely scale of change, IAB UK developed a series of ‘AI overlays’ that estimate the proportion of media spend by channel that is ‘AI-driven’, defined as a media buying and delivery decision made by an AI-powered algorithm. IAB UK forecasts most of the growth will come from the adoption of AI buying tools in search, such as Google’s AI Max and PMax; social, including Meta’s Advantage+; and display advertising through agentic programmatic buying.

items.title: DuckDuckGo launches no-AI search extensions after users reject Google's AI-first Search - TweakTown
items.summary: # DuckDuckGo launches no-AI search extensions after users reject Google's AI-first Search. ## Traffic to DuckDuckGo's no-AI search page tripled after Google's overhaul, and the company is now making it even easier to ditch AI search entirely. **TL;DR:** Following Google's major AI-driven Search update, many users have shifted to DuckDuckGo, whose no-AI search traffic tripled and app installs surged. However, a significant number of users have pushed back against the **AI-heavy** direction and, in the process, many have ended up at DuckDuckGo. Traffic to DuckDuckGo's No AI search page tripled after Google's announcement and has continued to surge. To keep the momentum going, the company has now launched new browser extensions for Chrome and Firefox that let users set its AI-free search page, noai.duckduckgo.com, as their default search engine. From there, the Duck.ai chatbot can be disabled entirely, and the app can be set to open with a plain search interface by default every time.

items.title: Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026 - The Robot Report
items.summary: # Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026. May 2026 was a busy month full of robotics news, topped off by last week’s Robotics Summit & Expo in Boston. The event brought together thousands of robotics experts to discuss the latest technology and industry advances. Here are the 10 most popular articles on *The Robot Report* in the past month. Subscribe to *The Robot Report*Newsletter and listen to *The Robot Report* Podcast to stay up to date on the latest robotics developments. Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production in May 2026. London-based Humanoid said it is working with Bosch to scale production after the successful completion of a joint proof of concept in March. It serves as the primary hub for NEO, the company’s humanoid robot designed to operate quietly—at a decibel level lower than a modern refrigerator—while navigating domestic spaces. If you’ve built a robotics company in the past 10 years, chances are you’ve at least flirted with the idea of robotics as a service, or RaaS.

items.title: ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling - The Robot Report
items.summary: # ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling. ANSCER Robotics provides autonomous mobile robots and software for industrial material handling. Aiming to capture a larger share of the American logistics market, deep-tech startup ANSCER Robotics has raised $5.4 million (₹45 crore) in Series A funding to scale its fleet of hybrid warehouse robots globally. The company said it is designing and building automation for the realities of modern manufacturing and logistics by helping enterprises improve productivity, safety, and operational efficiency at scale. According to ANSCER Robotics, this latest investment will accelerate its mission to build India’s AI-native industrial automation platform for factories and warehouses worldwide. “Naturally, we are expanding our markets and our product line, and the company will have a booth at Automate 2026,” Mark Messina, CEO of ANSCER Robotics Americas, told *The Robot Report*. “Our robots are designed and built for deployment in complex, rough environments — manufacturing floors and dock areas.”. Mike Oitzman is Senior Editor of WTWH's Robotics Group and founder of the Mobile Robot Guide.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: Reservoir’s Danny Bernstein says we’re ‘entering the golden age of robotics’ in agriculture - AgFunderNews
items.summary: # Reservoir’s Danny Bernstein says we’re ‘entering the golden age of robotics’ in agriculture. #### **Reservoir's Danny Bernstein with Western Growers CEO and president Dave Puglia. With the cracks in chemical-based crop protection widening, many are now contemplating the role of farm robotics in helping growers reduce reliance on these inputs. Danny Bernstein, CEO and managing partner of farm robotics incubator Reservoir, says this thinking is top of mind for many of the startups now operating out of the company’s campuses, which are employing everything from zapping weeds via electricity to controlling pests with UV light. California-based Reservoir itself has now expanded to two campuses—one in the Salinas Valley and one in Sonoma County—and plans to break ground on a third, in Central Valley, later this summer. (The deal with Western Growers is for the incubator side of the business.) We also have a management company that runs our fund, and that’s Reservoir VC.

items.title: China is so far beating U.S. in robotics race: Wedbush’s Dan Ives - CNBC
items.summary: Monday - Friday, 10:00 - 11:00 SIN/HK | 0400 - 05:00 CET. # China is so far beating U.S. in robotics race: Wedbush’s Dan Ives. Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities said China is emerging as a leader in the global robotics race, even as the broader AI competition between the U.S. and China intensifies. He says Nvidia dominates in AI chips, but that robotics could become China’s key advantage in the next phase of the AI revolution, describing the sector as a major area of demand and innovation.

items.title: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the ge
items.summary:
This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc.

More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage:

1) raw text (hard/effortful to read)
2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default
3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default
...4,5,6,...
n) interactive neural videos/simulations

Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://t.co/z21CP5iQfu

There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen.

TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML.

items.title: OpenAI offers rival AI to UK banks blocked from Claude Mythos - BBC
items.summary: OpenAI has offered nine major UK banks access to its cyber security AI tool GPT-5.5 Cyber, as its fierce rival Anthropic has blocked them in previews of its version, Claude Mythos. Both tools are designed to find hidden security weaknesses in digital systems and can outperform humans at some hacking and cyber-security tasks. Some finance ministers, central bankers and financiers have since expressed serious concerns about it, fearing the model could undermine the security of all financial systems. The AI Security Institute, which has tested both Anthropic's Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber, said in its report that they reached "a similar level of performance" in the tasks it set them. The UK banks which will now gain access to OpenAI's GPT 5.5 Cyber include Lloyds Banking Group, HSBC and Nationwide. OpenAI has been more generous with access to Cyber 5.5, opening it up in the EU and also to banks in Japan and Canada, among others.

items.title: OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness - MLQ.ai
items.summary: # OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness. * OpenAI launched Rosalind Biodefense, a two-track program giving vetted developers and U.S. government agencies free access to GPT-Rosalind for pandemic preparedness and biosecurity work [1]. * GPT-Rosalind outperforms GPT-5, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5.4 on internal benchmarks in chemistry, biochemistry, and experiment design [2]. OpenAI on May 29 launched Rosalind Biodefense, a program that provides free access to its specialized life sciences AI model, GPT-Rosalind, for government agencies, academic institutions, and nonprofit developers working on pandemic preparedness and biodefense [1]. The program operates on two tracks: a developer track sponsoring vetted teams building epidemiological models, early-detection tools, and screening systems, and a government track extending access to select U.S. federal agencies and allied partners for outbreak-response planning, diagnostics, and medical countermeasure development [3]. The Rosalind Biodefense program expands GPT-Rosalind's use from general life-science research into national security and public health applications, marking the first time OpenAI has offered a specialized model free of charge to government partners at this scale [3].

items.title: Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program - Axios
items.summary: # Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program. OpenAI is launching a tool to help develop new biodefense and pandemic preparedness capabilities, according to an announcement shared first with Axios. **Driving the news:** OpenAI's new Rosalind Biodefense Program will offer its GPT-Rosalind model for life sciences research to "trusted developers" operationalizing biodefense tools. * The company will sponsor access to the tool and provide launch support for epidemiological modeling, early detection, screening, preparedness, non-pharmaceutical interventions and other public-health work. * OpenAI is supporting organizations that it says span the life cycle of biological threats "from prevention and early detection to societal resilience and medical countermeasure development.". **What's next:** OpenAI said it briefed the White House and several federal agencies on its approach, and is in the process of involving public-health-focused federal agencies. * The company is also expanding access to GPT-Rosalind for select U.S. government and allied partners to support public health and biodefense missions.

items.title: OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.' - GIGAZINE
items.summary: # OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.'. James Ousmayer, Attorney General of Florida, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, alleging deceptive practices and damages. **Attorney General James Uthmeier Files First-in-the-Nation State-Led Lawsuit Against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman for Deceptive Practices and Harms to Floridians | My Florida Legal**. **OpenAI Sued by Florida's Attorney General Over AI Harms - WSJ**. On June 1, 2026, local time, Attorney General James Usmayer filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging that the company deliberately released ChatGPT to the public, including children, while concealing significant risks, suppressing internal safety warnings, and deceiving Florida residents about the true nature and dangers of the product. Attorney General Ousmayer explained, 'Today we announced the first state-led lawsuit in the nation against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman. > Today, we announced the first-in-the-nation state-led lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman.

items.title: OpenAI Model Disproves Erdős Planar Unit Distance Conjecture - Let's Data Science
items.summary: In a May 20 blog post, **OpenAI** announced that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem** conjectured by **Paul Erdős** in 1946, presenting an infinite family of point arrangements that give a polynomial improvement over prior grid constructions (OpenAI blog). OpenAI wrote that the proof was checked by a group of external mathematicians and released a companion paper with background and details (OpenAI blog; Scientific American). Prominent mathematicians quoted in coverage described the result as notable: **Daniel Litt** called it "the first result produced autonomously by an AI that I find interesting in itself" (ScienceAlert; Scientific American), and **Timothy Gowers** said no prior AI-generated proof had come close (Scientific American). **OpenAI** published a May 20 blog post announcing that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem**, a conjecture posed by **Paul Erdos** in **1946** (OpenAI blog).
